page: Amazon Web Services c8a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by AMD EPYC 9R45 processors. It features 192 physical cores and 192 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but features a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8a.metal-48xl demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidths, with uncached large-block reads reaching 1035.16 GB/sec and writes at 384.2 GB/sec. The processor's instruction set extensions, including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration (AES, VAES, SHA_NI), contribute to top-tier scores in Passmark CPU tests, database operations, and Redis SET operations, which registers 440.57 million operations per second.

The server is highly capable for large language model (LLM) prompt processing, achieving top-tier token throughput across small, medium, and large models. Text generation speeds are strong for medium and large models, though average for smaller models. Given the high compute density, lack of local storage, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, this bare-metal instance is optimized for compute-heavy workloads, high-throughput web serving, batch processing, and distributed memory-bound applications that leverage external storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9R45 processors running at 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, with no local storage or onboard GPUs. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmarks place this instance in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It supports advanced vector extensions (AVX-512) and cryptographic acceleration. This server is engineered for high-density compute workloads, large-scale web serving, Redis databases, and LLM prompt processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal compute-optimized server with 192 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and memory performance.

tagline: A high-density bare-metal compute platform featuring 192 AMD EPYC cores and DDR5 memory for demanding parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimized for compute-heavy workloads, Redis, and LLM prompt processing
